service.name: Discord API
service.description_for_ai: Communication platform API for gaming and developer communities. 500M+ registered users. Create bots that send messages, manage servers, handle slash commands. Dominant platform for developer communities, gaming, and OpenClaw community itself.

auth.how_to_get: Create app at https://discord.com/developers/applications → Bot → Token. Free.

capabilities.description_for_ai: Send a message to a Discord channel. Supports rich embeds with title, description, color, fields, and images. Use for notifications, alerts, or bot responses.

embeds.description: Rich embed objects with title, description, color, fields

capabilities.description_for_ai: Get information about a Discord server (guild): name, member count, channels, roles.
Send a message via Discord webhook — no bot token needed, just a webhook URL. Simplest way to post notifications to Discord. Use for CI/CD alerts, monitoring, or any one-way notification.

webhook_url.description: Full webhook URL from Discord channel settings

agent_hints.webhook_simplest: For one-way notifications use send_webhook — no OAuth, no bot setup. Get webhook URL from channel Settings → Integrations → Webhooks.
agent_hints.embed_color: Embed color is an integer. Blue=3447003, Green=5763719, Red=15548997, Yellow=16705372. Convert hex to decimal.
agent_hints.channel_id: Right-click any channel in Discord → Copy Channel ID (need Developer Mode enabled in Discord settings).
agent_hints.rate_limits: Discord has per-channel rate limits (5 req/5 sec). Space out messages to avoid 429 errors.
agent_hints.openclaw: OpenClaw community lives on Discord — this API is how OpenClaw bots post to channels.
